name: FAISS (Facebook AI Similarity Search)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Meta Research; C++ core with Python bindings

performance.notes: State-of-the-art ANN performance; GPU version 10-100x faster than CPU; billion-scale search

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors. Multiple index types: Flat (exact), IVF (inverted file, approximate), HNSW, PQ (product quantization), ScaNN. C++ core with Python bindings. GPU variants (faiss-gpu). Billion-vector scale. Used internally for recommendation systems and search. Lower-level than vector databases — no persistence, no metadata filtering, just fast ANN search. Foundation layer used by many vector DBs internally.

gotchas: API is C++-influenced — less Pythonic than modern alternatives
Index type selection requires understanding of tradeoffs
Not suitable as full vector database (build on top of it)
best_when: High-performance ANN search in Python without a full vector DB
Billion-scale similarity search with GPU acceleration
Research requiring precise control over index types and parameters
avoid_when: Production RAG systems (use Qdrant, Weaviate for metadata + search)
When persistence and metadata filtering are needed
Teams wanting managed vector search (use Pinecone)
summary: FAISS is Meta's high-performance similarity search library. C++ core, Python bindings. Supports multiple index types (Flat, IVF, HNSW, PQ) for billion-scale ANN search. GPU version 10-100x faster than CPU. The foundational ANN library used internally by many vector databases. No persistence or metadata — use when you need raw ANN search performance without the overhead of a full vector DB.
